name: Léopoldville
notes: One of six provinces created in the 1933 colonial reorganization. Named after the capital city. Contained the districts of Léopoldville, Bas-Congo, Kwango, and Lac Léopold II.

name: Lusambo
notes: Also known as Province du Kasai. Created in the 1933 reorganization from part of the former Congo-Kasaï province. Contained the districts of Sankuru and Kasai.

name: Coquilhatville
notes: Also known as Province de l'Équateur. Created from the former Équateur province in the 1933 reorganization. Contained the districts of Congo-Ubangi and Tshuapa.

name: Stanleyville
notes: Also known as Province Orientale. Created from part of the former Orientale province in the 1933 reorganization. Contained the districts of Stanleyville, Uele, and Kibali-Ituri.

name: Costermansville
notes: Also known as Province du Kivu. Created from part of the former Orientale province in the 1933 reorganization. Contained the districts of Kivu and Maniema. Renamed Kivu after independence.

name: Élisabethville
notes: Also known as Province du Katanga. Katanga was the first province created as a vice-government in 1910. Reorganized in 1933 with the districts of Tanganika, Lualaba, and Haut-Katanga.
